articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued
more.description: Despite efforts at stabilization and reduced inflation, over 90% of transactions in Zimbabwe are still conducted in US dollars. Inflation has dropped to single digits for the first time in nearly 30 years, though annual inflation slightly increased to 4.4% in March. Zimbabwe’s central bank governor says the country’s currency is significantly undervalued, arguing that […]The post Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work
more.description: HARARE — Health minister Douglas Mombeshora on Monday appealed to striking nurses to suspend their job action on the first day of a three-day walkout over pay. Nurses walked out at hospitals across the country, accusing the government of paying lip service to salary demands. They earn an average of US$290 and ZiG5,000 per month, […]The post Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zanu-PF Youth League Trashes Chivayo's U.S.$3.6 Million Donation - Says Parliament Should Not Be Bought
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] In a surprise turn of events, Zanu PF's Youth League has released a scathing statement trashing shadowy businessman Wicknell Chivayo's US$3.6 million donation to Parliament.

more.description: Source: Zimbabwe pushes for regionally harmonised education, examination standards – herald Sikhumbuzo Moyo, smoyo@chronicle.co.zw ZIMBABWE has called for a harmonised education system that also ring-fences against any leakage of examinations in any member State of the Southern African Development Community. Minister of Primary and Secondary Education, Torerayi Moyo, made the call on Friday as he addressed [...] The post Zimbabwe pushes for regionally harmonised education, examination standards appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Botswana President arrives today for BNC, ZITF
more.description: Source: Botswana President arrives today for BNC, ZITF – herald Nyore Madzianike, nyore.madzianike@zimpapers.co.zw BOTSWANA President Advocate Duma Boko is expected to arrive in Harare this afternoon ahead of the Fifth Session of the Zimbabwe-Botswana Bi-National Commission (BNC), which he will co-preside over with President Mnangagwa tomorrow. The high-level meeting is expected to underscore deepening bilateral cooperation [...] The post Botswana President arrives today for BNC, ZITF appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.description: Source: Government convenes urgent talks to resolve nurses’ strike – herald Rumbidzayi Zinyuke, rumbidzai.zvinyuke@gmail.co.zw GOVERNMENT has convened an extraordinary meeting between the Health Apex Panel and the Health Services Commission in a bid to resolve ongoing industrial action by nurses, amid growing concerns over service disruptions at public health institutions. more.title: ZITF 2026 ignites higher business prospects for local, foreign firms
more.description: Source: ZITF 2026 ignites higher business prospects for local, foreign firms – herald Rutendo Nyeve and Judith Phiri, Zimpapers Writers DOMESTIC and foreign exhibitors have expressed strong optimism for clinching lucrative deals and expanding markets from intense engagements at the 2026 Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF), which kicked off in Bulawayo yesterday with participants showcasing [...] The post ZITF 2026 ignites higher business prospects for local, foreign firms appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Tobacco exports earn Zimbabwe US$536m
more.description: Source: Tobacco exports earn Zimbabwe US$536m – herald Agriculture Reporter Zimbabwe has earned US$536 million from the export of 81 million kilogrammes of tobacco year to date, as the country continues to shift towards the shipment of value-added products of the crop. During the same period last year, merchants had exported 49,7 million valued at [...] The post Tobacco exports earn Zimbabwe US$536m appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zimbabwe moves to regulate social media and digital platforms
more.description: Zimbabwe plans to regulate the use of social media and digital platforms. Harare is drafting a new law that seeks to tighten oversight and combat what it calls an unchecked spread of misinformation and sharing of harmful material online.

more.title: Zimbabwe takes UNSC bid to SA
more.description: THE minister of Foreign Affairs, Amon Murwira, has taken Zimbabwe’s campaign for a non-permanent United Nations Security Council seat to South Africa...
